abstract: JSValue  compare(_:) Instance Methodcompare(_:)iOS 18.0+iPadOS 18.0+Mac Catalyst 18.0+macOS 15.0+tvOS 9.0+visionOS 2.0+func compare(_ other: JSValue) -> JSRelationConditionReturn ValueA value of JSRelationCondition, a kJSRelationConditionUndefined is returned if an exception is thrown.
overview:
JSValue  compare(_:) Instance Methodcompare(_:)iOS 18.0+iPadOS 18.0+Mac Catalyst 18.0+macOS 15.0+tvOS 9.0+visionOS 2.0+func compare(_ other: JSValue) -> JSRelationConditionReturn ValueA value of JSRelationCondition, a kJSRelationConditionUndefined is returned if an exception is thrown.

Compare two JSValues.

The result is computed by comparing the results of JavaScript’s ==, <, and > operators. If either self or other is (or would coerce to) NaN in JavaScript, then the result is kJSRelationConditionUndefined.

# compare(_:) | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ JavaScriptCore ](/documentation/javascriptcore)

-  compare(_:) 

Instance Method# compare(_:)

iOS 18.0+iPadOS 18.0+Mac Catalyst 18.0+macOS 15.0+tvOS 9.0+visionOS 2.0+

```
func compare(_ other: JSValue) -> JSRelationCondition
```

## [Return Value](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/compare(_:)-5w184#return-value)

A value of JSRelationCondition, a kJSRelationConditionUndefined is returned if an exception is thrown.

## [Discussion](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/compare(_:)-5w184#discussion)

Compare two JSValues.

The result is computed by comparing the results of JavaScript’s ==, <, and > operators. If either self or other is (or would coerce to) NaN in JavaScript, then the result is kJSRelationConditionUndefined.
